# Scenario 9: Version Ranges (What to Do When Gradle Chooses a Version You Didn’t Intend)

## What This Scenario Demonstrates
This scenario demonstrates Gradle's support for version ranges.

## Dependency Graph
`[1.0.0, 2.0.0)`

### Gradle (Groovy DSL)

```{.groovy include="/demos/gradle-demo/scenario-9-ranges/build.gradle" snippet="s9-deps"}
```

## Expected Intuition
`1.0.0`.

## Actual Resolution Results
*   **Gradle**: Resolves `1.0.0`.

## Classpath Reality
`1.0.0`.

## Why This Happens
Gradle supports Maven-style ranges.

## How Developers Commonly "Fix" This
N/A

## Safer Ways to Take Control
Locking.

## Signals to Watch For
N/A

## How This Scales in Real Systems
N/A

## Key Takeaway
Ranges work but are dynamic.

## Related Scenarios
*   [Scenario 11: Locking](../scenario-11-locking/guide.md)
